name: Ya Baqi
meaningBlocks: Root B-Q-Y denotes existing continuously without end, surviving eternally after all else perishes.
The One whose connection is the only thing that passionately remains when this fragile world violently shatters.
Guarantees that our righteous deeds become everlasting, securing our eternal dwelling in His highest Paradise.
dua: Ya Baqi, when everything I love in this fleeting, fragile world eventually perishes, let my connection to You passionately remain. Make my righteous deeds everlasting in my record, and grant me an eternal, everlasting dwelling in Your highest Paradise.

name: Ya Rahman
meaningBlocks: Derived from the root R-H-M, meaning womb, indicating a nurturing, deeply maternal compassion.
Refers to Allah's universal mercy that encompasses all creation, regardless of their belief.
Reminds believers that His boundless grace sustains all earthly existence and life.
dua: Ya Rahman, from Your throne You wrote mercy upon Yourself. You were merciful before there was anyone to receive mercy. Embrace every part of me with that mercy—my past with its scars, my present with its needs, my future with its uncertainties. Let me taste the mercy You keep for those who return. Reward me with the mercy I have yet to know, and raise me to the highest place in Paradise despite my lows. Cover me from storms I don't see coming. Mend me before I break. Spare me from trials of every kind. Let Your mercy reach me in ways I'll only understand when I finally meet You.

name: Ya Wahid
meaningBlocks: Rooted in W-H-D, meaning absolute uniqueness and indivisibility.
Signifies that Allah is utterly alone in His essence, without partners or equals.
Anchors the core Islamic principle of Tawhid, directing all pure devotion solely to Him.
dua: Ya Wahid, the One my heart was created to seek. Pull me away from every false worship, from anything that divides my devotion, until nothing fills my longing but You. Unify my pursuits into a single direction, and when the world pulls me apart, center me in Your oneness. Open my heart to see that nothing compares, so that I'm never overly impressed by anyone but You, and I come to love Your names and attributes in a way that frees me from all others but You.

name: Ya Witr
meaningBlocks: Refers to that which is odd-numbered or strictly singular.
Emphasizes that Allah is unmatched, independent, and completely unparalleled.
Spiritually encourages believers to seek Him in solitude, matching His uniqueness with undivided worship.
dua: Ya Witr, when I stand alone at the close of the night, let my solitude remind me of Your singular majesty. Make my heart distinct by its devotion, and make me one of Your rarest servants.

name: Ya Hadi
meaningBlocks: From H-D-Y, meaning to guide, direct, or show the way gently.
Denotes the One who provides innate physical guidance to creation and spiritual guidance to hearts.
Reassures believers that true direction through life's darkness comes strictly from His light.
dua: Ya Hadi, guide me when my heart forgets the way. Pull me back from every turn that leads me astray. Let Your guidance feel closer than the doubts in my chest.

name: Ya Rashid
meaningBlocks: Root R-Sh-D indicates moving in the right direction with supreme wisdom.
Represents the Unerring Director who leads creation to what is ultimately beneficial.
Inspires believers to align their natural instincts with righteousness and truth.
dua: Ya Rashid, teach me to see truth instinctively. Make faith beloved to me, make righteousness feel natural, and let sin feel heavy on my chest and hated by my every breath. Inspire me with wisdom that protects me even from myself.
meaning: The Guide to the Right Path

name: Ya Nur
meaningBlocks: Means the literal and spiritual Light that illuminates the heavens and the earth.
Signifies the One who brings hearts out of the darkness of ignorance and doubt.
Reminds us that faith itself is a divine light granted to those seeking clarity.
dua: Ya Nur, light every darkness I carried or carry within. Let Your light pour into my heart and onto all my other limbs until You make me a light, and everything I see reminds me of You.

name: Ya Mubin
meaningBlocks: From B-Y-N, meaning to be clear, manifest, and perfectly distinct.
The One who makes the truth obvious and clears away all falsehood and ambiguity.
Encourages believers to look for His clear signs in the universe and within themselves.
dua: Ya Mubin, make Your signs clear in what surrounds me and what is within me. Reveal the lessons hidden in every trial. Let me never lose sight of Your signs again.

name: Ya Waliyy
meaningBlocks: Root W-L-Y means to be close, to manage, and to lovingly protect.
Denotes Allah as the ultimate guardian and ally of the believers.
Provides profound comfort that those who trust Him are never truly alone or undefended.
dua: Ya Waliyy, be my closest friend when the world drifts away. Guard me with the grip that never slips. Keep me firm on the ground of Your goodness. Make my faith steady when my heart trembles. Let me love what brings me to Your stability, and make me patient with what keeps me on its shore.

name: Ya Rafeeq
meaningBlocks: From R-F-Q, denoting ultimate gentleness, kindness, and compassionate companionship.
Highlights that Allah acts with softness in His decrees and loves gentleness in His servants.
Comforts the lonely by assuring them of His subtle, tender presence in their lives.
dua: Ya Rafeeq, be tender with my soul as You unfold Your plan. Soften the path without removing its purpose. Make me gentle with others as You've been gentle with me. And when I am lonely, fill that space with Your company.

name: Ya Rafi'
meaningBlocks: Root R-F-' means to lift up, elevate, or raise in status.
The One who honors the righteous by raising their ranks in this life and the next.
Teaches that true elevation comes only through sincere humility before Him.
dua: Ya Rafi', raise me the way You raise every seed from the soil. Shape my growth with all You continue to give. Let every season You place me in become a lesson in Your care.

name: Ya Mawla
meaningBlocks: Shares the root W-L-Y, emphasizing supreme lordship, mastery, and protective care.
Indicates the ultimate Patron who takes full charge of our affairs when we surrender.
Assures the believer that having Allah as a protector makes all worldly fears insignificant.
dua: Ya Mawla, be my protector when I have no one else to stand up for me. Take charge of my affairs when I lose control of them. Hold me near when fear surrounds me, and make Your guardianship always enough for me.

name: Ya Nasir
meaningBlocks: From N-S-R, meaning to grant support, aid, and ultimate victory.
The One who assists the oppressed and grants triumphs that no earthly power can stop.
Reminds believers that true victory is entirely dependent on His unseen divine support.
dua: Ya Nasir, help me in every battle I cannot see. Grant me triumph without arrogance, and victory only in a way that brings me closer to You.

name: Ya Sayyid
meaningBlocks: Means the ultimate Lord and Chief possessing absolute authority over all.
Denotes that He is the only One truly worthy of absolute, unconditional servitude.
Strips away human arrogance, reminding us that true dignity lies in being His servant.
dua: Ya Sayyid, teach my heart that true honor is to only serve You. Let my dignity be in humility, my leadership in obedience, and my freedom in surrender.

name: Ya Malik
meaningBlocks: Root M-L-K indicates supreme sovereignty, ownership, and dominion.
The absolute King who commands the universe and to whom all rises and falls belong.
Cultivates deep gratitude by reminding us that everything we "own" is merely loaned by Him.
dua: Ya Malik, You are the King of everything I know and hold. Remind me that nothing I hold is truly mine, and that every rise and fall belongs to Your decree. Let my gratitude expand with every breath You lend to me.

name: Maliki Yawmid-Deen
meaningBlocks: Specifically designates Allah as the sole authority on the final Day of Reckoning.
Highlights that on that Day, all earthly kingships will completely dissolve before Him.
Urges believers to settle their debts and live with intense accountability for the Hereafter.
dua: Maliki Yawmid-Deen, the Owner of the Day when debts are paid, teach me to settle my dues before I meet You. Keep me honest with what I owe, and hopeful in what You forgive.
meaning: Owner of the Day of Judgment

name: Al-Malik
meaningBlocks: Emphasizes the inescapability of His absolute, eternal rule over all creation.
Reminds humanity that His sovereignty is perfect, needing no validation from His subjects.
Humbles the rebellious heart, fostering complete contentment under His divine decree.
dua: Ya Malik, the King whose command no one can escape, let Your sovereignty humble my pride. Make me content under Your rule, and protect me from a rebellious heart that would cause me to fall out of Your favor.

name: Ya Jalil
meaningBlocks: From J-L-L, meaning to be supreme, magnificent, and profoundly great.
The One whose immense glory inspires overwhelming awe and deep reverence.
Refines the believer's character by teaching them to stand small, yet dignified, before His majesty.
dua: Ya Jalil, fill my heart with reverence that draws me near, not fear that drives me away. Let my awe of You refine me until I stand before You small but never disgraced.

name: Ya Khaliq
meaningBlocks: Root Kh-L-Q means to measure, proportion, and bring into existence from nothingness.
Signifies the deliberate, purposeful fashioning of every single atom in the universe.
Instills profound wonder, reminding us that every breath is an intentional act of His creation.
dua: Ya Khaliq, You brought me out of nothingness into being. Remind me that my existence itself is a mercy, that every breath, every cell, and every chance is a deliberate act of Your creation.

name: Ya Badi'
meaningBlocks: From B-D-', meaning to invent or create completely without any prior model or precedent.
The One who uniquely designs the universe and flawlessly repairs what is broken.
Gives hope that He can forge entirely new, beautiful beginnings out of dead ends.
dua: Ya Badi', the One who perfects what He produces, repair what I've broken within myself. Make me whole again with the same precision by which You fashioned all of Your creation.

name: Ya Musawwir
meaningBlocks: Root S-W-R means to form, shape, or give distinct features.
The One who individually sculpts our inner traits and outer appearances in the womb.
Encourages believers to seek inner beauty (character) that matches His perfect outer creation.
dua: Ya Musawwir, You shaped me in the unseen, gave me form, face, and story. Beautify my character as You've beautified my features. Let what You see within me be more pleasing than what others see outside of me.

name: Ya Mubdi'
meaningBlocks: Means the One who initiates all existence and originates life effortlessly.
Represents the continuous power to create fresh openings and realities for His servants.
Comforts those who feel stuck, assuring them that He can always begin things anew.
dua: Ya Mubdi', keep creating new openings when I run out of ways. Create for me new beginnings when I rush to my end, and new realities when I feel doomed by my sins.

name: Ya Jabbar
meaningBlocks: (Also Name 22) Focuses here on His power to perfectly rebuild what oppression has shattered.
The Restorer who tenderly heals the fractured hearts of the ummah traumatized by worldly cruelty.
Ensures that when He allows us to break, it is solely to reconstruct us into something stronger.
dua: Ya Jabbar, mend what their hands have broken. Rebuild the lands they've shattered, and heal the hearts that tremble for You under their bombs and their boots. Set right the fractures in our Ummah, and make every wound a doorway back to You. When You allow us to break, let it only be to rebuild us stronger. And when You heal us, let the healing only bring us nearer.

name: Ya Samad
meaningBlocks: Means the ultimate, solid foundation upon whom all of existence entirely depends.
The One who requires no sustenance, rest, or assistance from anyone or anything.
Cures spiritual emptiness by teaching that wholeness is only found in depending on Him.
dua: Ya Samad, the One who needs nothing while everything needs You, make me whole by depending only on You. Fill the hollowness inside of me until no desire within me competes with Your glory.

name: Ya Haqq
meaningBlocks: Root H-Q-Q denotes absolute reality, rightness, and that which is undeniable.
The unshakeable Reality that exposes and collapses all earthly lies and deceptions.
Serves as the ultimate anchor, keeping the believer grounded in eternal, enduring truth.
dua: Ya Haqq, make Your truth the anchor of my heart and my guiding path to Your light. Let every lie I chase collapse before Your reality, and every truth I seek lead me to Your next promise. Keep me grounded in what endures, and turn my eyes away from what deceives.

name: Ya Wasi'
meaningBlocks: From W-S-', meaning vast, expansive, and limitless in capacity.
Describes His boundless knowledge, endless mercy, and limitless provision.
Expands the human heart to hold more patience, vision, and gratitude than we thought possible.
dua: Ya Wasi', expand my heart to hold more gratitude, my patience to endure with more grace, and my vision to see beyond my limits.

name: Ya Ghaffar
meaningBlocks: Root Gh-F-R means to continuously cover, protect, and pardon.
The One who forgives repeatedly, no matter how many times a servant falters.
Eradicates despair, ensuring that shame never blocks the path of returning to His mercy.
dua: Ya Ghaffar, forgive me for every single sin I've ever put forth. Cover me in this life and the next, and don't let shame keep me from returning to You. For every time I fall and rise again, meet me with another chance. Let my heart grow softer with every repentance, and my view of the door of Your mercy only grow wider, for You have never tired of forgiving me despite my constant sins. Forgive me for what I know and what I don't, what I've done and what I still may do, and turn the stains of my past into light on my scrolls. Reach me with Your forgiveness even in the holes that I've dug for myself.

name: Ya Tawwab
meaningBlocks: From T-W-B, meaning to return repeatedly.
The One who constantly turns toward His servants in mercy so they can turn to Him in repentance.
Makes seeking forgiveness a joyful, constant companion rather than a burdensome chore.
dua: Ya Tawwab, turn towards me so that I may turn back to You, and accept me every time I return. Make repentance my constant companion and joy, and my reward in finding You again.

name: Ya Wadud
meaningBlocks: Root W-D-D signifies an active, expressive, and deeply affectionate love.
Represents the One who continuously shows love through abundant, often unnoticed blessings.
Inspires believers to find immense sweetness in His love, even amidst life's difficult tests.
dua: Ya Wadud, love me even after I falter, and let Your love reach me through every mercy I overlook. Teach me to love You more than what You give, and to find sweetness in every test that draws me closer to Your love. Announce Your love for me in the heavens until I hear that announcement myself in Paradise. Surround me with it in everything around me, and let me feel it deep inside myself.

name: Ya Muhsin
meaningBlocks: From H-S-N, meaning ultimate beauty, excellence, and perfection in action.
The One who perfects everything He touches and gives with absolute grace.
Motivates believers to perform deeds with Ihsan (excellence) as a reflection of His perfection.
dua: Ya Muhsin, You perfect everything You touch. Make me one of those who do good because You do good. Then let my deeds of excellence to others be a reflection of Your unmatched perfection.

name: Ya Shakur
meaningBlocks: Root Sh-K-R means to acknowledge, multiply, and immensely reward.
The One who abundantly rewards even the smallest, most broken efforts of His servants.
Reminds us that the very ability to be grateful is itself a profound gift from Him.
dua: Ya Shakur, for every small effort I make, reward me beyond measure. Let me never grow tired of thanking You, and never forget that gratitude itself is a gift from You. Accept from me what is broken and multiply it until it becomes whole.

name: Ya Sami'
meaningBlocks: Means the One who hears every whisper, thought, and loud declaration flawlessly.
Listens intimately to the unspoken prayers hidden deep within the chest.
Assures the believer that their calls are never ignored, unheard, or forgotten.
dua: Ya Sami', You hear every sound before it's spoken. Inspire in me the words that are pleasing to You. Make sincere the dua that I utter between breaths, and even the ones I make loud alongside others. Let me never doubt that You are listening, and never tire of my calling.

name: Ya Qarib
meaningBlocks: Root Q-R-B denotes physical or spiritual proximity and intimate closeness.
The One who is nearer to us than our own jugular vein, fully aware of our internal states.
Provides intense comfort, cutting through the noise and isolation of the external world.
dua: Ya Qarib, You are nearer than every thought I hide. Let me feel Your nearness in the stillness of my prayer. Let the clarity and comfort of Your closeness cut through all the noise outside, and forgive me for what should have never made it inside.

name: Ya Mujib
meaningBlocks: From J-W-B, meaning to answer, reply, and actively respond.
The One who fulfills needs with perfect wisdom, often giving what is best rather than what is asked.
Protects the heart from ever doubting that an answer is on its way.
dua: Ya Mujib, answer not only what I ask, but what I mean. Give me what I would have prayed for if I knew what was best for me. When I raise my hands to You, fill them only with what draws me closer to You, and protect me from ever believing that an answer is not possible or questioning You.

name: Ya Mu'min
meaningBlocks: Root A-M-N signifies safety, security, and the granting of absolute trust.
The source of unshakeable faith that secures the heart against worldly fears and anxieties.
Guards the soul from the whispers of doubt, making trust in Him heavier than any worry.
dua: Ya Mu'min, grant me faith that no fear can shake. Secure my heart in Your promise, and guard my soul from every whisper that breeds doubt. Let my trust in You be heavier than any worry that visits me.

name: Ya Raqib
meaningBlocks: From R-Q-B, meaning to observe closely, guard diligently, and remain vigilant.
The ever-watchful Guardian who never sleeps, protecting us in our most vulnerable moments.
Cultivates Taqwa (mindfulness), knowing we are constantly beneath His protective gaze.
dua: Ya Raqib, the Watchful One who never sleeps, watch over me in the moments that no one else can, and be my shield from even what I see but cannot change. Preserve my faith through trial and time. Guard my loved ones when they are far away from me. Keep safe the blessings I forgot to thank You for, and protect me, for without You I cannot protect myself.

name: Ya Wakil
meaningBlocks: Root W-K-L means to entrust a matter entirely to a capable manager.
The ultimate Disposer of Affairs who perfectly handles whatever we release into His control.
Relieves the immense burden of worldly anxiety by taking charge of what we cannot carry.
dua: Ya Wakil, I entrust to You my every matter—what I seemingly can handle, and that which is obviously beyond my control. Take charge of what confuses me, and carry what I cannot hold. Make Your plan more beloved to me than even my own, for You are enough for me no matter what else I own.

name: Ya Basir
meaningBlocks: Means the One who completely sees the seen and the unseen, the physical and the spiritual.
Witnesses every hidden tear, unnoticed effort, and silent act of good.
Brings deep comfort to those who feel invisible or unappreciated by the rest of the world.
dua: Ya Basir, You see what no one else sees—every tear I hide, every effort unnoticed, every quiet of good. Let me find comfort in being seen by You when I feel invisible to the rest of the world.

name: Ya Shahid
meaningBlocks: From Sh-H-D, meaning to testify, be present, and observe with absolute awareness.
The ultimate Witness to the purity of our intentions when others misjudge us.
Instills a consciousness that drives the believer to live and die truthfully under His sight.
dua: Ya Shahid, be my witness when my intentions are pure, and when they falter, let my fear of You set them aright. Let me live conscious that You are always truly watching, and die as one who lived truthfully under Your sight.

name: Ya Hafiz
meaningBlocks: Root H-F-Z means to guard, protect, and maintain carefully.
The One who preserves our physical well-being, our loved ones, and most importantly, our faith.
Acts as our vital shield, safeguarding the blessings we frequently forget to thank Him for.
dua: Ya Hafiz, watch over my heart when it drifts, and over my thoughts before they turn to deeds. Make me mindful of You in secret and in public, until awareness of You becomes my protection from slip.

name: Ya Sattar
meaningBlocks: From S-T-R, meaning to drape, cover, or hide completely.
The One who graciously veils our private flaws, sins, and shameful acts from public disgrace.
Allows repentance to remain a dignified, private affair between the servant and the Lord.
dua: Ya Sattar, cover my faults with the same grace You always have. Hide my flaws even as You heal them. Let my repentance be quiet and my shame sincere, and my forgiveness made complete beneath Your protective veil.

name: Ya Subhan
meaningBlocks: Means the One who is utterly transcendent, perfect, and completely free of any defect.
Reminds us that He is entirely untouched by the chaos, pain, and heaviness of the worldly life.
Pulls the believer out of spiritual darkness through the sheer power of His absolute perfection.
dua: Ya Subhan, how perfect You are beyond everything I can imagine. When the world feels loud and heavy, remind me that You are untouched by its chaos. Let my glory and praise of You rise above the darkness even if I drown myself in it, and pull me out of it by Your perfection even after I dragged myself down.

name: Ya Quddus
meaningBlocks: Root Q-D-S denotes absolute holiness, purity, and flawless sanctity.
The One who is entirely free from the flaws and limitations of human nature.
Purifies the believer's heart from pride, resentment, and anything that clouds the soul.
dua: Ya Quddus, purify me inside and out. Cleanse my worship of pride, my heart of resentment, my soul of anything that clouds its way back to You, and make me worthy of the nearness I seek to You.

name: Ya Salam
meaningBlocks: Means the ultimate source of safety, tranquility, and flawless peace.
The One who sends absolute calm into the internal storms and anxieties of the human chest.
Transforms believers into sources of peace for others until they enter His eternal Home of Peace.
dua: Ya Salam, send peace into every storm within my chest. Let Your calm settle where fear once lived. Make me a source of peace for others until I meet You in the Home of Peace and get to hear from You Yourself.

name: Ya Ghani
meaningBlocks: Root Gh-N-Y implies complete independence, richness, and requiring absolutely nothing.
The One who possesses all things while creation remains constantly impoverished and in need of Him.
Cures human greed by enriching the heart with deep contentment and relying solely on His wealth.
dua: Ya Ghani, You are free of all need while I am always in need of You. Let me never beg from those as poor as me. Make me rich through contentment, and fill my heart with gratitude instead of greed.

name: Ya Hamid
meaningBlocks: From H-M-D, meaning to praise with love, reverence, and profound gratitude.
The One who is intrinsically worthy of all praise, regardless of whether creation praises Him or not.
Teaches the believer to echo gratitude deeply during both moments of crushing hardship and joyful ease.
dua: Ya Hamid, You are praised whether or not I remember to say it. Let my praise echo in hardship as it does in ease, and let every breath I take be a quiet thank you.

name: Ya Fattaah
meaningBlocks: Root F-T-H means to open, conquer, and grant decisive victory or profound clarity.
The One who miraculously opens locked doors, softens hardened hearts, and reveals hidden solutions.
Inspires unshakeable trust that He will forge paths to goodness where we currently only see solid walls.
dua: Ya Fattaah, open doors I cannot find and hearts that I cannot reach. Open paths to good where I only see walls, and open my chest to trust whatever You've decreed. Let every opening You grant me lead me back to You.

name: Ya Muqaddim / Ya Mu'akhkhir
meaningBlocks: Represent His ultimate control over time, sequence, and the unfolding of destiny.
Advancing brings us forward for our benefit; delaying holds us back for our absolute protection.
Teaches profound patience, ensuring we feel perfectly content and never misplaced in His timeline.
dua: Ya Muqaddim, Ya Mu'akhkhir, when You withhold, let me not lose hope. And when You expand, let me not lose humility. Tighten my path only to redirect me to Yours, and widen it in ways that only increase my gratitude to You. Teach me to find You in both the squeeze and the release, and to love You both in the stillness and the flow. When You bring me forward, make me worthy of the moment. And when You hold me back, make me patient with the waiting. Advance me only when the step will please You, and delay me only when the pause will protect me. Wherever I stand in Your decree, let me always feel content that I am never misplaced.

name: Ya Alim
meaningBlocks: From '-L-M, meaning infinite, absolute, and perfectly comprehensive knowledge.
The One who knows our hidden secrets, our past mistakes, and our unwritten futures.
Grants comfort that His intimate knowledge of us is more merciful than people's superficial judgments.
dua: Ya Alim, You know what I hide and what I show, what I've done and what I will do. Let what You know of me be better than what people know of me, and forgive me for what only You know.

name: Ya Hakeem
meaningBlocks: Root H-K-M implies perfect judgment, precise action, and placing things exactly where they belong.
The One whose profound wisdom completely surrounds and justifies the things we cannot comprehend.
Demands trust in His perfect timing and the unseen beauty unfolding within our difficult trials.
dua: Ya Hakeem, Your wisdom surrounds what I cannot understand. When I question the purpose, remind me of Your perfection. Grant me the patience to trust Your timing, and the insight to see the beauty unfolding in Your decree.

name: Ya Latif
meaningBlocks: From L-T-F, denoting extreme gentleness, imperceptible subtlety, and meticulous care.
The One who fulfills our needs and sends kindnesses through details we often completely overlook.
Reaches the deepest, most delicate corners of our hearts that no human words could ever touch.
dua: Ya Latif, work gently through the details of my life. Send kindnesses that I may not notice until I look back, but can still feel at every moment. Let Your subtle care reach the corners of my heart that no words or people could ever touch.

name: Ya Khabir
meaningBlocks: Root Kh-B-R implies deep, inner awareness of the true nature and hidden reality of all things.
The One who fully perceives our deepest fears, subtle flaws, and hidden intentions.
Urges internal purification, ensuring our private reality matches our public facade before we meet Him.
dua: Ya Khabir, You are fully aware of what's within me—my fears, my flaws, and my hidden intentions. Purify what You see inside of me until it is pleasing to You, and let nothing inside me here contradict what will be produced when I stand before You.

name: Ya Qahhar
meaningBlocks: From Q-H-R, meaning to overpower, conquer, and utterly break tyrannical resistance.
The Dominator who crushes the arrogance of oppressors and subdues all false earthly powers.
Serves as a stern warning against internal arrogance, lest we resemble the oppressors He subdues.
dua: Ya Qahhar, the One who subdues all false powers, subdue every tyrant who raises a hand against the innocent. Break the arrogance of those who spill the blood of Your beloved servants. Let their cruelty be crushed by Your justice. Subdue also the arrogance within me so that I never resemble the oppressor in any form.

name: Ya Aziz
meaningBlocks: Root '-Z-Z denotes immense strength, noble dignity, and complete invincibility against any defeat.
The One who is far too glorious to ever let injustice permanently prevail over the innocent.
Promises to strip tyrants of false power while clothing the humble and oppressed in eternal dignity.
dua: Ya Aziz, You are too glorious for injustice to prevail, too mighty for oppression to last. Before You, show the tyrants that no throne stands forever, and honor the humble who still call Your name even beneath the rubble. Clothe our people in dignity when the world strips them bare, raise them in rank, and protect them from despair.

name: Ya Qadir
meaningBlocks: From Q-D-R, meaning to possess absolute capability, perfect measure, and unstoppable execution.
The One who overturns the darkest despair, revives the dead, and turns earthly rubble into resurrection.
Reminds those who boast of military or financial might of their sheer insignificance before His power.
dua: Ya Qadir, nothing is beyond Your power as You part the seas and revive the dead. Lift our people from beneath the weight of this world. Show us that no injustice is too deep for You to overturn, and no despair too dark for You to light again. Every decree rests in Your command. Only You are able to turn tears into triumph and rubble into resurrection. Your power is perfect, Your execution without flaw. Let those who boast of might see how small they really are. Crush the arrogance that claims control over Your creation, and remind us that even the most helpless among us are upheld by You alone. Grant us strength without cruelty, faith without fatigue, and victory that only humbles us before You until we dwell in that place where our oppressors can no longer reach us, and only those You love stand before You.

name: Ya Adl / Ya Muqsit
meaningBlocks: Roots indicate ultimate equity, fairness, and the perfect balancing of all accounts.
The One who merely delays the cruel but is never unjust by even the weight of a single atom.
Demands that believers passionately love justice, act with equity, and purify themselves from internal oppression.
dua: Ya Adl, Oh You whose balance never tips unfairly, when the world feels rigged for cruelty, remind us that You are merely delaying the cruel and never unjust, even by the weight of a single atom. Make us witnesses to Your justice in this life, and part of its fulfillment in the next. And let us love justice ourselves and be purified from oppression within. Restore to the victims what was stolen from them, and to the weary hearts what hope they lost. Make us a people who act with equity and never swerve from accountability.

name: Ya Hakam
meaningBlocks: Root H-K-M signifies the ultimate arbiter who delivers clear, decisive, and unstoppable verdicts.
The Supreme Judge who will inevitably establish the truth when the corrupt courts of men utterly fail.
Forces believers to evaluate their lives, ensuring they always stand firmly on the side of the oppressed.
dua: Ya Hakam, You are the Judge when the courts of men fail. When truth is buried beneath lies, let Your verdict rise clear and unstoppable. Judge between us and those who wrong us without remorse, and let us not find ourselves except on the side of the oppressed.

name: Ya Hasib
meaningBlocks: From H-S-B, meaning to meticulously calculate, audit, and serve as the ultimate sufficiency.
The One who precisely accounts for every unseen tear shed and every silent cruelty endured in fear.
Delivers swift reckoning for the oppressor and gentle, immense reward for those who patiently endured.
dua: Ya Hasib, You are enough when proof is hidden and power is abused. Account for every tear that fell unseen and every cruelty that was silenced by fear. Let Your reckoning come swift for those who harm, and ever so gently for those who endured.

name: Ya Razzaq
meaningBlocks: Root R-Z-Q means to grant physical sustenance, spiritual nourishment, and continuous provision.
The One who continuously feeds every creature in ways they cannot see or fully comprehend.
Ensures our trust in His guaranteed sustenance heavily outweighs our worldly anxieties about the future.
dua: Ya Razzaq, You provide for every creature in ways we cannot see. Feed my heart before my hands, and let my trust in Your sustenance outweigh my fear of the future. Provide for me in ways that bring me closer to You, and never let what I earn make me forget who it's from.

name: Ya Karim
meaningBlocks: From K-R-M, indicating noble generosity, immense dignity, and giving effortlessly without restriction.
The One who grants incredible blessings far beyond what our flawed actions actually deserve.
Inspires believers to mimic His nobility by giving constantly, gracefully, and without any expectation.
dua: Ya Karim, You give beyond what I deserve, and with a dignity too noble for me to live up to. Let Your generosity flow through me as a reflection of how it flows from You. Make my giving noble and my gratitude constant. When You withhold, remind me that You are still giving, even if in ways I may not yet understand.

name: Ya Akram
meaningBlocks: The superlative form of Karim, meaning the absolute highest, unmatched level of generosity.
The One who honors us with ultimate contentment and an abundant portion in the eternal Hereafter.
Creates a life of profound gratitude for a Lord who always gives infinitely more than we can earn.
dua: Ya Akram, the most generous of those who give, honor me with contentment in every decree. Make my share of this world enough, and my portion of the next abundant. Let me live in gratitude for Your generosity that I can never live up to, and die with praise for a Lord who always gave more.

name: Ya Qawiyy
meaningBlocks: Root Q-W-Y implies inexhaustible, perfect strength that knows no fatigue or weakness.
The One who firmly anchors believers when the crushing weight of the world threatens to bend them.
Teaches us to humbly draw all our strength from Him, completely avoiding the trap of self-reliant pride.
dua: Ya Qawiyy, strengthen me where I am weak, and make me firm where I bend too easily. Lift me so that I can lift others without ever believing I lifted myself. When the weight of the world feels heavier than my faith, remind me that Your strength is the only way to see through it. Let me draw strength from Your power, but never be weakened by my pride.

name: Ya Matin
meaningBlocks: From M-T-N, meaning steadfast, solidly unshakeable, and possessing intense durability.
The One whose power and promises remain absolutely steady when everything else in life violently shakes.
Anchors faith so deeply into the heart that no worldly loss or tragedy can ever uproot it.
dua: Ya Matin, make me steady when everything else shakes. Let my faith be anchored deep enough so that no loss can uproot it. Keep my footing secure in moments that test my balance.

name: Ya Mu'izz
meaningBlocks: Root '-Z-Z indicates granting true dignity, respect, and elevation of status.
The One who proves that true honor is found exclusively in humbling oneself before His majesty.
Protects the soul from the exhausting, fragile pursuit of validation from other human beings.
dua: Ya Mu'izz, grant me the honor that comes only from You. Let my dignity rest in humility before You, my worth be measured only in worship of You. Protect me from chasing validation from those as fragile as I, and make my highest victory the achievement of Your pleasure.

name: Ya Shafi
meaningBlocks: From Sh-F-Y, meaning to cure completely, providing physical and spiritual remedies.
The One who removes bodily sickness while simultaneously expelling the toxic poison of sin from the soul.
Transforms physical aches into profound reminders of His mercy and pathways back to His closeness.
dua: Ya Shafi, heal me in ways that medicine can't fix. Remove whatever sickness has touched my body, and expel along with it any sin that poisoned my soul. Let every ache remind me of Your mercy, and every cure draw me closer to You. Heal this Ummah from its wounds, and make us whole again through faith and patience.

name: Ya Tayyib
meaningBlocks: Root T-Y-B signifies that which is wholesome, pure, perfectly clean, and free of defects.
The One who is absolutely pure and structurally only accepts from His servants that which is pure.
Demands the strict purification of our wealth, food, and intentions from the unlawful and show-off.
dua: Ya Tayyib, make my life pure enough to be accepted by You. Purify my wealth from the unlawful and my worship from showing off. Purify my clothes and my food in every way that I seek You.

name: Ya Mu'ti
meaningBlocks: From '-T-Y, meaning to actively hand over, bestow, and grant freely.
The Ultimate Source of every single thing that heals, sustains, and brings joy to the human experience.
Reminds us that every earthly gift is fundamentally a blessed invitation to turn back to the Giver.
dua: Ya Mu'ti, Giver of all that heals and all that sustains, let me never forget that every gift from You is a blessed call back to You, and let it find me only as a grateful recipient and servant.

name: Ya Sabur
meaningBlocks: Root S-B-R implies immense restraint, endurance, and delaying immediate, deserved reactions.
The One who patiently gives us endless time to repent rather than striking us down for our immediate sins.
Teaches the believer to embody patience over base desires, through difficult trials, and during painful delays.
dua: Ya Sabur, teach me the patience that doesn't expire. Make me calm when delay tests me, and grateful even as I wait. Grant me patience over my desires, and through my trials, and upon the deeds that will protect me from the Fire.

name: Ya Haleem
meaningBlocks: From H-L-M, meaning to be gentle, clement, and to graciously overlook mistakes despite having power.
The One who watches our repetitive slips yet continues to cover them in incredible kindness.
Compels believers to be highly forgiving and profoundly forbearing with the flaws of those around them.
dua: Ya Haleem, You see my mistakes and still give me time to fix them. You watch my slips and still cover them in kindness. Make me forbearing with those around me, and quick to forgiveness with those who may wrong me.

name: Ya Aliyy
meaningBlocks: Root '-L-W denotes absolute elevation, supreme loftiness, and transcending all creation.
The One who is infinitely higher than everything that confuses, defeats, or causes despair in us.
Lifts the believer's gaze above earthly pettiness, resentment, and the pursuit of low, temporary status.
dua: Ya Aliyy, raise my heart above resentment and smallness. Lift my gaze towards the higher ways that reach You, and place my hopes in what lasts with You. Let my character rise even when my status falls, for You are higher than everything that confuses me or defeats me. Lift me above pettiness, above despair, above sin, until my soul finds its peace in Your elevation.

name: Ya Kabir
meaningBlocks: From K-B-R, implying absolute, unmatched greatness in essence, rank, and majesty.
The One whose sheer magnitude forces total humility and permanently evicts human arrogance.
Guides our devotion strictly to Him, as anything else we pursue shrinks into nothingness before His greatness.
dua: Ya Kabir, how small I am before You. Let humility settle where arrogance once lived. Remind me that greatness belongs only to You and can only be realized through You. Shut the doors to what diminishes me, and guide my devotion to what fills me through You.

name: Ya Azim
meaningBlocks: Root '-Z-M means to be colossal, awe-inspiring, and staggeringly magnificent.
The Lord of infinite might whose reverence heavily outweighs any fear we have of His creation.
Stills our most unstable, anxious moments through deep, grounding trust in His absolute magnificence.
dua: Ya Azim, my Lord of might without measure, make my reverence for You heavier than any fear of creation. Let my heart bow before You long before my body does, and let my most unstable positions be stilled by my trust in You.

name: Ya Muta'ali
meaningBlocks: An intensified form of Aliyy, emphasizing transcendence far beyond any human comprehension or flaw.
The One who is infinitely higher than the temporary, fleeting illusions and desires we constantly chase.
Elevates the soul's ambition, making our ultimate goal worthy of His profound nearness.
dua: Ya Muta'ali, You are higher than every desire I chase. Lift me from the illusions of this world, and make my ambitions worthy of Your nearness. Let my soul rise to You with sincerity and longing until I meet You in the highest of places.

name: Ya Majeed
meaningBlocks: From M-J-D, denoting vast honor, absolute splendor, and overwhelmingly abundant nobility.
The Glorious One whose infinite greatness entirely humbles and eclipses all worldly forms of glory.
Ensures that a servant's highest rank and greatest title is simply the honor of remembering and serving Him.
dua: Ya Majeed, the Glorious whose greatness humbles all glory, honor me by keeping me close. Let my meeting with You there be an extension of my meetings with You here. Make my remembrance of You my greatest title, and my servitude to You my highest rank.

name: Ya Jawad
meaningBlocks: Root J-W-D means to give magnanimously, open-handedly, and with immense, flowing liberality.
The One who actively grants us profound blessings before we even realize we need to ask for them.
Instructs believers to mimic His hidden generosity by giving to others without any expectation of return.
dua: Ya Jawad, Your generosity reaches me before I even raise my hands. You give before I ask, and more than I deserve. Let me never mistake Your gifts for my earning. Make my giving a small reflection of Yours—open-handed, unseen, and without expectation.

name: Ya Mannan
meaningBlocks: From M-N-N, indicating the initiation of immense, unearned favors and sheer grace.
The One who deliberately wrote kindness and provision into our stories long before we could ever thank Him.
Shifts our perspective so we view blessings not as entitled rights, but as loving, divine invitations.
dua: Ya Mannan, every blessing I have is a favor You began. You wrote kindness into my story long before I could thank You. Let me recognize Your favors not as entitlement, but as invitation.

name: Ya Wahhab
meaningBlocks: Root W-H-B means to give massive, continuous gifts utterly without condition, reason, or limit.
The One who gifts profound faith, unexpected openings, and unconditional forgiveness solely out of love.
Sustains our hope, allowing us to confidently seek miracles from Him when we cannot see a logical way forward.
dua: Ya Wahhab, the Giver of gifts without reason or limit, grant me what only love can grant: forgiveness without condition, and openings without explanation. Gift me faith that doesn't falter when I fall, and hope that keeps finding You even when I can't see the way.

name: Ya Afuww
meaningBlocks: From '-F-W, meaning to completely obliterate, wipe away, and leave absolutely no trace behind.
The One who erases the very existence of our sins, unlike mere forgiveness which covers them.
Clears our dark records completely, turning past shame into the fertile soil for sincere, lifelong devotion.
dua: Ya Afuww, erase what I've done in the mercy that only You can write. Wipe away the traces of every sin that followed me into this night, until I stand before You as if I'd never fallen. Let my shame become the soil from which sincerity grows, and my repentance the rope that pulls me back to You. You love to pardon, so pardon me completely. Forgive what I remember and what I've forgotten, what I've confessed and what I've concealed. Make my record as clean as the day You created me, and my heart lighter than it has ever been before. Let me walk out of this night free of what once chained me, hopeful in what awaits me, and humbled by the Lord who forgives simply because He loves to.

name: Ya Hayyiyy
meaningBlocks: Root H-Y-Y denotes profound bashfulness, modesty, and beautiful divine shyness.
The One who is incredibly shy to ever turn away the raised, empty hands of His pleading servant.
Inspires sheer awe that the Lord of the Universe feels shy to reject our flawed, unworthy requests.
dua: Ya Hayyiyy, You are too shy to turn away these hands. How many times have I asked with nothing worthy in return, and yet You still answered in a way that left me in awe? Forgive me for my mountains of fault for just a droplet of Your mercy, and give me more than I dare to ask because of Your generosity.

name: Ya Jami'
meaningBlocks: From J-M-', meaning to bring together, unite, and perfectly assemble what is scattered.
The One who gathers the broken pieces of our hearts and reunites loved ones separated by time and distance.
Guarantees the ultimate gathering of all humanity on the Day of Judgment beneath the shade of His throne.
dua: Ya Jami', gather the pieces of my heart that this world has broken, or perhaps I myself have broken. Unite what distance and time have separated. Bring together my past and my purpose, my loved ones and the reward that You've promised, until we meet again under the shade of Your throne.

name: Ya Warith
meaningBlocks: Root W-R-Th means to remain after others are gone and to inherit all that was temporarily loaned.
The One to whom all wealth, power, and creation permanently returns when the worldly life ends.
Comforts believers that their sincere deeds and legacy are eternally preserved by Him, even when forgotten by men.
dua: Ya Warith, as everything returns to You, what fades for me remains preserved with You. Keep my deeds when I am gone and let them grow, and my legacy when I'm forgotten, so long as not by You. Inherit my soul with Your pleasure, and leave behind only what You'll love to return to me for when I meet You.

name: Ya Awwal
meaningBlocks: Means the absolute First, existing eternally before any beginnings were ever written or created.
The One who intimately knew our names and destinies long before our very first breath.
Reminds us that every single good endeavor must begin with Him and rely entirely on His permission.
dua: Ya Awwal, You were there before beginnings were written. Before my first breath, You already knew my name. Remind me that nothing starts without You, and that every good I begin is only by Your permission. Let my first step in anything always be toward You.

name: Ya Akhir
meaningBlocks: Means the absolute Last, enduring eternally and flawlessly after everything else completely fades away.
The ultimate, permanent destination when every worldly name, empire, and monument is utterly forgotten.
Focuses the believer's ultimate goal: ensuring their last breath is in His remembrance and upon His mercy.
dua: Ya Akhir, You remain when all else fades. When every name is forgotten, Yours endures. Let my last breath be in Your remembrance, and my last sight be upon Your mercy.

name: Ya Zahir
meaningBlocks: From Z-H-R, meaning to be apparent, overt, and brilliantly evident through clear signs.
The One whose immense majesty is visibly undeniably woven into the beauty of the sun, moon, and nature.
Transforms everything the believer witnesses in the physical universe into a direct doorway back to Him.
dua: Ya Zahir, You are evident in every beauty, in the rise of the sun and the appearance of the moon. Let me see Your majesty in all that surrounds me, until everything I witness becomes a door back to You.

name: Ya Batin
meaningBlocks: Root B-T-N means to be internal, unseen, and intimately close to the core of everything.
The One who is nearer to us than our pulse, fully knowing the deep pain and thoughts we cannot articulate.
Preserves our spiritual reality in the unseen, granting an intimate nearness that outlasts our time on earth.
dua: Ya Batin, You are nearer than my thoughts, closer than my pulse. You know the things I can't say, and forgive the things I can't hide. Preserve me in the unseen as You do in the open, and let my nearness to You outlast my time on this earth.

name: Ya Allah
meaningBlocks: (Reflects the comprehensive plea utilizing the Supreme Name to encompass the totality of His Mercy).
Reminds the soul that He was merciful from the very beginning and will remain merciful at the absolute end.
Serves as the ultimate, desperate plea to be returned to Him securely in the company of His beloved Prophet ﷺ.
dua: Ya Allah, when I rise from one of Your seasons of incredible mercy, let me remember that You were always merciful from the beginning, and You will always be in the end, so long as I return to You. Ya Allah, return me to You. Ya Allah, return me to You. Ya Allah, return me to You, in the presence of Your beloved (Sallallahu Alayhi Wasallam) and all those who knew You the way I want to. In the beginning, and the end, and always to You, all praise is due.

name: Ya Hayy
meaningBlocks: Root H-Y-Y denotes perfect, eternal, and absolute life that never experiences fatigue, sleep, or death.
The One who constantly breathes vital life into our fading worship and resurrects our lost sense of purpose.
Keeps our internal faith fiercely alive when the external world floods us with dying, deceptive promises.
dua: Ya Hayy, the Ever-Living who never fades, put life into my worship when it starts to fade, and life into my purpose when I start to feel lost again. Let my faith in You stay alive when the world floods me with dying promises, and the devil deceives with his deception.

name: Ya Qayyum
meaningBlocks: From Q-W-M, meaning the absolute Maintainer who independently holds all of existence firmly together.
The One who physically and spiritually sustains us, preventing us from falling apart in our darkest moments.
Demands we rely entirely on His continuous grace, knowing we cannot survive even for the blink of an eye without it.
dua: Ya Qayyum, the Sustainer of all that exists, hold me together when I fall apart. Sustain my heart with Your remembrance and my deeds with sincerity. Do not leave me to myself, even for the blink of an eye. Steady me when I swerve with Your guidance. Receive me when I return with Your grace.

name: Ya Rahim
meaningBlocks: Shares the root R-H-M but denotes a specific, continuous, and highly focused mercy.
Represents the special, enduring compassion reserved specifically for the believers in the Hereafter.
Wraps the struggling soul in tenderness, carrying us through our daily slips and spiritual heaviness.
dua: Ya Rahim, wrap me in Your special mercy reserved for the believers. When my deeds fall short and my heart grows heavy, let Your compassion carry me through. Forgive my daily slips and embrace me with the tenderness that only You can provide.

name: Ya Muhaymin
meaningBlocks: Means the ultimate Protector, the Overseer, and the One who determines the truth of all things.
The vigilant Guardian who constantly protects our highly vulnerable faith from unseen whispers and doubts.
Provides intense spiritual security, keeping our internal state pure long after our own guard has dropped.
dua: Ya Muhaymin, stand guard over my vulnerable heart. Protect my faith from the whispers of doubt, and my soul from the unseen harms of this world. Keep my internal state pure under Your watchful care, long after I have let my own guard down.

name: Ya Mutakabbir
meaningBlocks: From K-B-R, denoting the only One who rightfully possesses absolute supremacy, pride, and supreme greatness.
Serves to violently rid the human heart of its own toxic arrogance, entitlement, and false pride.
Teaches that true honor for a human is found strictly in lowering oneself entirely to His supreme majesty.
dua: Ya Mutakabbir, rid my heart of its own arrogance and pride. Remind me of my absolute smallness before Your supreme majesty, and let me find true honor in lowering myself entirely to You. Protect me from ever looking down on another soul.

name: Ya Bari'
meaningBlocks: Root B-R-' means to meticulously evolve, form, and bring creation into its perfect, functioning state.
The One who takes our inherent brokenness and deliberately molds it into profound spiritual beauty.
The ultimate curer of deep spiritual ailments, reforming us into the ideal servants we were created to be.
dua: Ya Bari', You evolve everything from nothingness to its perfect state. Take my brokenness and mold it into something beautiful. Cure my soul of its deepest spiritual ailments, and reform me into the servant You created me to be.

name: Ya Qabid
meaningBlocks: From Q-B-D, meaning to tightly withhold, restrict, or draw inward with absolute purpose.
The One who deliberately restricts worldly provisions or paths specifically to protect the soul from harm.
Squeezes the human heart solely to expel the toxic love of this fleeting world from residing within it.
dua: Ya Qabid, when You restrict my provisions or my paths, let me know it is only to protect me. Squeeze my heart only to expel the love of this fleeting world from it, and let every restriction be a redirection to what is truly better for me.

name: Ya Basit
meaningBlocks: Root B-S-T means to expand, widen, grant generously, and unroll provision effortlessly.
The One who gracefully expands our chests to receive difficult decrees with unshakeable peace.
Extends wealth and lifespan strictly so they may be utilized as bridges of charity toward His pleasure.
dua: Ya Basit, expand my chest to gracefully receive Your decree. Expand my provision so that it may be a means of immense charity, and extend my lifespan only if it allows me to gather more deeds that bring me closer to You.

name: Ya Khafid
meaningBlocks: From Kh-F-D, meaning to lower, demean, or drastically reduce the rank of the arrogant.
The One who inevitably lowers the ranks of worldly oppressors who violently spread corruption on earth.
Keeps our personal egos deeply lowered in this life, thereby protecting us from humiliation in the next.
dua: Ya Khafid, lower the ranks of the oppressors who spread corruption on this earth. Lower my own ego so that I never act arrogantly over anyone else, and let me be humbled in this life so that I am not humiliated in the next.

name: Ya Mudhill
meaningBlocks: Root Dh-L-L denotes the active stripping of honor, bringing profound disgrace and humiliation.
The One who ultimately humiliates falsehood and those who arrogantly masquerade their cruelty as truth.
Serves as a plea to shield our own private sins from becoming the source of our public earthly disgrace.
dua: Ya Mudhill, humiliate the falsehood that masquerades as truth. Protect me from ever being disgraced in this life or the Hereafter, and never let my private sins become the source of my own public humiliation.

name: Ya Ghafur
meaningBlocks: From Gh-F-R (like Ghaffar) but emphasizes the immense volume and depth of His continuous forgiveness.
The One who completely blankets our most overwhelming, repetitive shortcomings with His endless pardon.
Destroys the whispers of despair, proving that no soul has ever strayed too far to be forgiven by Him.
dua: Ya Ghafur, forgive the sins I keep repeating out of my own weakness. Blanket my overwhelming shortcomings with Your endless pardon, and let me never despair of Your immense forgiveness, no matter how far I have strayed.

name: Ya Muqit
meaningBlocks: Root Q-W-T means to physically sustain, spiritually nourish, and provide exact, needed strength.
The One who continuously nourishes the soul with deep faith just as He feeds the physical body daily.
Sustains our fragile devotion during times of severe spiritual starvation, preventing us from seeking Haram.
dua: Ya Muqit, nourish my soul with deep faith just as You nourish my body with daily provision. Sustain my devotion in times of spiritual starvation, and let me never hunger or thirst for anything that displeases You.

name: Ya Ba'ith
meaningBlocks: From B-'-Th, meaning to awaken, revive, dispatch, and physically raise from the dead.
The One who will undoubtedly raise every physical body from the graves for ultimate accountability.
Prayed to for the immediate resurrection of our deadened hearts and sleeping consciences before our time runs out.
dua: Ya Ba'ith, revive my dead heart just as You will raise the dead from their graves. Awaken my conscience before my time runs out, and resurrect my soul on the Day of Judgment among those You love most.

name: Ya Muhsi
meaningBlocks: Root H-S-Y means to count, calculate, and meticulously enumerate every single detail without missing an atom.
The One who keeps an exact, flawless count of every breath taken, leaf fallen, and deed performed.
Instills profound mindfulness, urging us to beg His grace to overlook the countless idle moments we accumulate.
dua: Ya Muhsi, You keep count of every breath I take and every leaf that falls. Let my counted deeds be ones of sincere worship, and by Your ultimate grace, overlook the countless mistakes and idle moments I have accumulated.

name: Ya Mu'id
meaningBlocks: From '-W-D, meaning to repeat, bring back, and perfectly restore to an original state.
The One who gently brings our wandering souls safely back to the straight path after we have strayed.
Restores the lost innocence of our early faith and revives our crushed hope when all seems completely lost.
dua: Ya Mu'id, restore to me the innocence and sweetness of my early faith. Whenever I wander far away, bring me back to Your path gently. Restore my hope when everything around me feels completely lost.

name: Ya Muhyi
meaningBlocks: Root H-Y-Y (like Hayy) meaning to actively bestow life, grant vitality, and revive the lifeless.
The One who breathes vibrant life into our withering intentions and our spiritually broken Ummah.
Ensures that our hearts beat dynamically with nothing but profound love, awe, and longing for Him.
dua: Ya Muhyi, breathe life into my good intentions before they wither away. Give life to our broken Ummah, and let my heart beat vibrantly with nothing but deep love and longing for You.

name: Ya Mumit
meaningBlocks: From M-W-T, meaning to decree the end of worldly life and transition the soul.
The One who ultimately commands our death, a necessary threshold to meet Him in the eternal Hereafter.
Called upon to ensure our final moments are peaceful, upon Islam, and uttering the Shahada.
dua: Ya Mumit, let me die upon Islam and sincere faith. When my appointed time arrives, let my departure be peaceful, my final words be the Shahada, and let my grave become a spacious garden from the gardens of Paradise.

name: Ya Wajid
meaningBlocks: Root W-J-D means to find, possess everything, and entirely lack absolutely nothing.
The totally un-impoverished Lord who has everything perfectly within His grasp and divine presence.
Reassures the deeply lost soul that absolute comfort, richness, and true belonging are found in Him alone.
dua: Ya Wajid, You possess all things and lack absolutely nothing. Help me find my way when I am utterly lost, and let me find all my comfort, richness, and belonging in You alone.

name: Ya Majid
meaningBlocks: From M-J-D, indicating brilliant, shining nobility, honor, and flawless generosity.
The One whose sheer brilliance and noble character completely outshines the darkness of our base desires.
Inspires believers to elevate their own characters, demanding dignity and generosity in their dealings with others.
dua: Ya Majid, elevate my character with true nobility. Let the brilliance of Your glory outshine my base desires, and make me generous, dignified, and noble in my daily dealings with all of Your creation.

name: Ya Ahad
meaningBlocks: Root A-H-D signifies absolute, indivisible oneness in essence, fundamentally distinct from "Wahid".
The One who has no parents, no offspring, no partners, and is completely peerless in every way.
Demands the strict purification of our intentions, ensuring our worship is totally unshared with human praise.
dua: Ya Ahad, You are uniquely One, without any partner, equivalent, or peer. Purify my intentions so that they are uniquely for You alone, completely unshared with the desire for anyone else's praise or validation.

name: Ya Muqtadir
meaningBlocks: From Q-D-R, denoting the absolute power to determine, strictly measure, and flawlessly execute all outcomes.
The One who holds the exact measure of our destinies when we feel entirely powerless against our circumstances.
Anchors the anxious heart, forcing absolute trust in His capability to decree a beautiful, unseen destiny.
dua: Ya Muqtadir, You hold the ultimate measure of all things. When I feel entirely powerless against my circumstances, anchor my heart to Your absolute capability. Decree for me a beautiful destiny that I cannot yet see.

name: Ya Barr
meaningBlocks: Root B-R-R means to be deeply devoted, profoundly kind, and the absolute source of pure righteousness.
The One who rains down pure, unadulterated goodness, making every earthly blessing a bridge to Paradise.
Inspires deep dutifulness in believers, particularly toward their parents and in kindness to all His creation.
dua: Ya Barr, rain down Your pure goodness upon my life. Make me dutiful to my parents, deeply kind to Your creation, and let every single goodness I receive become a bridge that leads me straight to Your pleasure.

name: Ya Muntaqim
meaningBlocks: From N-Q-M, meaning to deliver exact, necessary retribution and uncompromising justice against severe evil.
The One who actively avenges the innocent, the slaughtered, and the oppressed who cry out in the night.
Instills profound fear in tyrants and begs protection from ever becoming the cause of another's pain.
dua: Ya Muntaqim, avenge the innocent and the oppressed who cry out to You in the dead of the night. Bring justice upon the tyrants who have no fear of You, and protect me from ever being the oppressor or the cause of someone else's pain.

name: Ya Ra'uf
meaningBlocks: Root R-A-F signifies an intense, warming, pitying tenderness that goes even beyond standard mercy.
The One whose compassion is infinitely softer, warmer, and more protective than a mother's embrace.
Cushions our most devastating spiritual and worldly falls with His endless, incredibly gentle grace.
dua: Ya Ra'uf, Your compassion is softer and warmer than a mother's embrace. Deal with my profound flaws with Your ultimate tenderness, and cushion my many falls with Your endless, gentle grace.

name: Ya Malik-ul-Mulk
meaningBlocks: A compounded name asserting absolute, eternal ownership over the concept of dominion itself.
The One who casually grants sovereignty to whom He wills and violently strips it from whom He wills.
Strips away our false, exhausting illusion of control, demanding we become humble, loyal servants in His vast kingdom.
dua: Ya Malik-ul-Mulk, You give sovereignty to whom You will and strip it from whom You will. Make me a loyal, humble servant in Your vast kingdom, and strip away any false sense of control I stubbornly cling to in my life.

name: Ya Dhu-al-Jalal wa-al-Ikram
meaningBlocks: Combines absolute terrifying majesty (Jalal) with overwhelming, beautiful generosity (Ikram).
The One who entirely humbles us with His immense greatness, yet relentlessly showers us with His grace.
Prayed to so that He may bless our faces to be among those worthy of looking upon His face in the Hereafter.
dua: Ya Dhu-al-Jalal wa-al-Ikram, shower me relentlessly with Your generosity, and humble me entirely with Your majesty. By Your grace, make this face of mine one that is blessed to look upon Yours in the Hereafter.

name: Ya Mughni
meaningBlocks: Root Gh-N-Y indicates the active granting of wealth, deep sufficiency, and the removal of all need.
The One who enriches the starving soul with absolute, unshakeable internal contentment.
Frees the believer from the exhausting, endless poverty of earthly greed and constant social comparison.
dua: Ya Mughni, enrich my soul with absolute contentment. Free me from the exhausting poverty of greed and comparison, and make me the wealthiest of people in my complete reliance on You alone.

name: Ya Mani'
meaningBlocks: From M-N-', meaning to firmly block, prohibit, protectively withhold, and fiercely defend.
The One who actively prevents harm from reaching our families and stops us from achieving harmful desires.
Reassures us that heavily blocked paths are actually His divine interventions keeping us away from severe sin or ruin.
dua: Ya Mani', withhold from me anything—no matter how much I desire it—that will draw me away from You. Prevent harm from reaching my family, and strongly block the pathways of sin before my feet can even step onto them.

name: Ya Darr
meaningBlocks: Root D-R-R acknowledges that all distress, harm, and tribulation fundamentally originate from His ultimate decree.
The One who controls adversity, proving that absolutely no harm can touch us except by His exact permission.
Teaches us to utilize forced distress and earthly trials as rigorous tools for our ultimate spiritual elevation.
dua: Ya Darr, no harm can ever touch me except by Your decree. Protect me from the trials and harms of this world, and let any distress I am forced to endure be an ultimate purification and elevation for my soul.

name: Ya Nafi'
meaningBlocks: From N-F-', meaning the ultimate source of all benefit, usefulness, and profound earthly advantage.
The One who ensures the knowledge we seek and the deeds we perform bring ultimate benefit in the Hereafter.
Inspires believers to become profound sources of immense benefit, goodness, and light to everyone they interact with.
dua: Ya Nafi', bring ultimate benefit to my life and my Hereafter through the knowledge I seek and the deeds I perform. Make my presence on this earth a profound source of benefit and light to everyone I meet.
meaning: The Creator of Good / The Propitious
